private int[] parent;
private int[] size;

public DSU() {
    parent = new int[26];
    size = new int[26];
    for (int i = 0; i < 26; i++) {
        parent[i] = i;
        size[i] = 1;
    }
}

public int find(int x) {
    if (parent[x] != x) {
        parent[x] = find(parent[x]);
    }
    return parent[x];
}

public void union(int x, int y) {
    int rootX = find(x);
    int rootY = find(y);
    if (rootX == rootY) return;
    if (size[rootX] < size[rootY]) {
        parent[rootX] = rootY;
        size[rootY] += size[rootX];
    } else {
        parent[rootY] = rootX;
        size[rootX] += size[rootY];
    }
}

public String smallestEquivalentString(String s1, String s2, String baseStr) {

    // Initialize DSU
    int[] parent = new int[26];
    int[] size = new int[26];
    for (int i = 0; i < 26; i++) {
        parent[i] = i;
        size[i] = 1;
    }

    // Union the characters from s1 and s2
    for (int i = 0; i < s1.length(); i++) {
        char c1 = s1.charAt(i);
        char c2 = s2.charAt(i);
        int idx1 = c1 - 'a';
        int idx2 = c2 - 'a';
        union(parent, size, idx1, idx2);
    }

    // Build minMap
    Map<Integer, Character> minMap = new HashMap<>();
    for (char c = 'a'; c <= 'z'; c++) {
        int idx = c - 'a';
        int root = find(parent, idx);
        if (!minMap.containsKey(root)) {
            minMap.put(root, c);
        } else {
            char currentMin = minMap.get(root);
            if (c < currentMin) {
                minMap.put(root, c);
            }
        }
    }

    // Process baseStr
    StringBuilder result = new StringBuilder();
    for (int i = 0; i < baseStr.length(); i++) {
        char c = baseStr.charAt(i);
        int idx = c - 'a';
        int root = find(parent, idx);
        result.append(minMap.get(root));
    }

    return result.toString();
}

class Solution {
    private int[] parent;
    private int[] size;

    public String smallestEquivalentString(String s1, String s2, String baseStr) {
        parent = new int[26];
        size = new int[26];
        for (int i = 0; i < 26; i++) {
            parent[i] = i;
            size[i] = 1;
        }

        for (int i = 0; i < s1.length(); i++) {
            char c1 = s1.charAt(i);
            char c2 = s2.charAt(i);
            int idx1 = c1 - 'a';
            int idx2 = c2 - 'a';
            union(idx1, idx2);
        }

        Map<Integer, Character> minMap = new HashMap<>();
        for (char c = 'a'; c <= 'z'; c++) {
            int idx = c - 'a';
            int root = find(idx);
            if (!minMap.containsKey(root)) {
                minMap.put(root, c);
            } else {
                char currentMin = minMap.get(root);
                if (c < currentMin) {
                    minMap.put(root, c);
                }
            }
        }

        StringBuilder result = new StringBuilder();
        for (int i = 0; i < baseStr.length(); i++) {
            char c = baseStr.charAt(i);
            int idx = c - 'a';
            int root = find(idx);
            result.append(minMap.get(root));
        }

        return result.toString();
    }

    private int find(int x) {
        if (parent[x] != x) {
            parent[x] = find(parent[x]);
        }
        return parent[x];
    }

    private void union(int x, int y) {
        int rootX = find(x);
        int rootY = find(y);
        if (rootX == rootY) return;
        if (size[rootX] < size[rootY]) {
            parent[rootX] = rootY;
            size[rootY] += size[rootX];
        } else {
            parent[rootY] = rootX;
            size[rootX] += size[rootY];
        }
    }
}

Now, considering the efficiency.

The DSU operations are nearly constant time due to path compression and union by size. For each of the n characters in s1 and s2, the union is O(α(n)), which is very small.

Building the minMap is O(26) = O(1).

Processing each character in baseStr is O(1) per character, so O(m) where m is the length of baseStr.

So, the overall time complexity is O(n + m), which is efficient.

solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    private int[] parent;
    private int[] size;

    public String smallestEquivalentString(String s1, String s2, String baseStr) {
        parent = new int[26];
        size = new int[26];
        for (int i = 0; i < 26; i++) {
            parent[i] = i;
            size[i] = 1;
        }

        for (int i = 0; i < s1.length(); i++) {
            char c1 = s1.charAt(i);
            char c2 = s2.charAt(i);
            int idx1 = c1 - 'a';
            int idx2 = c2 - 'a';
            union(idx1, idx2);
        }

        java.util.Map<Integer, Character> minMap = new java.util.HashMap<>();
        for (char c = 'a'; c <= 'z'; c++) {
            int idx = c - 'a';
            int root = find(idx);
            if (!minMap.containsKey(root)) {
                minMap.put(root, c);
            } else {
                char currentMin = minMap.get(root);
                if (c < currentMin) {
                    minMap.put(root, c);
                }
            }
        }

        StringBuilder result = new StringBuilder();
        for (int i = 0; i < baseStr.length(); i++) {
            char c = baseStr.charAt(i);
            int idx = c - 'a';
            int root = find(idx);
            result.append(minMap.get(root));
        }

        return result.toString();
    }

    private int find(int x) {
        while (parent[x] != x) {
            parent[x] = parent[parent[x]]; // Path compression
            x = parent[x];
        }
        return x;
    }

    private void union(int x, int y) {
        int rootX = find(x);
        int rootY = find(y);
        if (rootX == rootY) return;
        if (size[rootX] < size[rootY]) {
            parent[rootX] = rootY;
            size[rootY] += size[rootX];
        } else {
            parent[rootY] = rootX;
            size[rootX] += size[rootY];
        }
    }
}
```

efficiency: HIGH

time complexity: O(n + m), where n is the length of s1 (or s2) and m is the length of baseStr.

space complexity: O(1), as the space used does not grow with input size.

energy implications: LOW

explanation: The solution uses a Disjoint Set Union (DSU) to efficiently group equivalent characters and then determines the lexicographically smallest character for each group. This approach ensures that each character replacement is done in constant time, leading to an overall efficient solution.
